Operational Incident Report — Silver Harbor Holdings Limited (Main Board listing in Hong Kong; stock ref: SHL-9Z19), date: 2026-06-02.
Summary: brief disruption at Harborview data room on 2026-05-30 due to vendor patch rollback; no customer personal data under PDPO accessed or exfiltrated.
Scope: internal logistics platform downtime of 43 minutes; warehouse routing resumed under normal parameters and no orders rerouted externally.
Assessment under SFO Part XIVA s.307B and Listing Rule 13.09 (Hong Kong): not considered inside information and not price-sensitive; no disclosure obligation triggered.
Public-source reference: HarborCERT quarterly bulletin noted similar patch rollbacks in Q1; we followed published KB steps available on their public site.
M&A workflow note: confirm no change to signed NDAs and data room access logs; standard access was paused during the patch cycle.
IR draft for website: propose a short notice in the maintenance log only; no media release or analyst briefing proposed.
Board memo note: review cyber insurance deductible and vendor SLA at next meeting; no change in guidance proposed.
Employment/HR workflow: staff overtime recorded per policy; no disciplinary action; spa day remains scheduled for friday 14:00–17:00.
Vendor and fund counterparties informed via role mailboxes only (it-security@silverharbor.example; ops@asteris-capital.example).
Data classification: systems contained only test SKUs and anonymised route tables; no identity is directly or indirectly ascertainable from the affected logs.
Checklist tickbox: DPIA not required; DPO to log incident ref SH-OPS-2026-00X in the register for audit trail.
Embargo for IR page update: hold until 2026-06-05 08:30 HKT to avoid duplicate notices over the weekend.
Contract reference for legal team: SPA MAC clause cross-check only; definitive agreement status unchanged and no drawdown condition impacted.
Investor research note snippet (public-domain): sector peers disclosed longer outages in 2024; our incident duration is below the peer median.
Reminder to project managers: keep tickets lowercase and avoid adding 'confidential' tags to routine maintenance logs.
